name: Everything Will Be Fine!
short_description: A dark conversation with a girl during a nightly car trip. A short multi-ending novel game where you try to understand this girl and her pain.
detailed_description:
This is a short multi-ending novel game where you talk to a girl during while taking a drive by night. Talk to her, listen to her, and try to understand her.

The Story

You wake up in a car running on an empty road at night.

Besides you is a girl. Out of the windows, a light rain is falling down on the asphalt.

Don't worry about where you are or where you're headed. Just keep talking to her.

Take a journey inside the heart of a struggling young girl.

The Game

- Choose dialogue options to advance the conversation

- Get one of four endings depending on your choices

- After seeing all four endings, you'll get a new conclusion

(Powered by "CRIWARE".CRIWARE is a trademark of CRI Middleware Co., Ltd.)

reviews.review_text:
Tried many times and still cant get to END5...(NOW CLEARED!)
Overall a good short VN with great atmosphere, music fit the scene well, maybe just too short (and too much time on the final puzzle)


[b]Spolier for END5:[/b]


[spoiler]I've unpacked the game,and the result is is that you will see a font difference(also slightly larger) in each choice when you start a new game after clear all 4 ends

if your choice is correct, the difference will continue,but if you get it wrong,the fonts will become the same[/spoiler]

Also some advice:[spoiler]I spent like 2 hour playing and feelling really annoyed, I think this need to be fixed to become more obivious or add more guidance to the puzzle so that any player can have a good experience[/spoiler]

Everything Will Be Fine! is a tightly contained, emotionally driven visual novel that builds its entire experience around a single conversation, using minimal mechanics and a confined setting to explore themes of vulnerability, communication, and quiet unease. Developed by nanohana and published by room6 under the yokaze label, the game unfolds almost entirely during a nighttime car ride, where the player interacts with a young girl through a series of dialogue choices. Rather than presenting a traditional narrative with clear structure or progression, it focuses on the subtleties of human interaction, allowing meaning to emerge gradually through tone, pauses, and implication.

The gameplay is deliberately simple, centered on selecting dialogue responses that shape the direction and emotional weight of the conversation. There are no puzzles, no resource systems, and no external objectives—only the act of listening and responding. Each choice influences how the girl reacts, subtly altering the flow of the interaction and leading to different endings. These variations are not defined by dramatic plot shifts, but by changes in emotional tone and understanding. The experience feels less like navigating a branching storyline and more like engaging in a delicate exchange, where even small decisions carry significance.

What gives the game its impact is the way it handles its subject matter. The conversation gradually reveals deeper layers of the girl’s thoughts, hinting at loneliness, anxiety, and unresolved emotional struggles. The writing avoids direct exposition, instead relying on fragmented dialogue and indirect expression. This creates a sense of ambiguity that invites interpretation, encouraging players to read between the lines and consider what is being left unsaid. The tension comes not from external conflict, but from the uncertainty of how to respond—whether to comfort, question, or simply listen. This emotional focus makes the experience feel intimate and, at times, quietly unsettling.

The visual presentation supports this tone through a restrained and minimalist style. The setting remains largely confined to the interior of the car, with the outside world fading into darkness. Character expressions are subtle but effective, conveying shifts in mood without exaggerated animation. Lighting plays an important role, with soft illumination contrasting against the surrounding night to create a sense of isolation. The limited scope of the visuals reinforces the feeling that the player is locked into this moment, unable to escape the conversation or its implications.

Sound design is equally understated, relying on ambient noise and a subdued soundtrack to maintain atmosphere. The hum of the road, the quiet rhythm of the environment, and the occasional use of music all contribute to a sense of immersion. Silence is used deliberately, allowing pauses in the dialogue to carry emotional weight. These moments can feel uncomfortable, emphasizing the uncertainty and fragility of the interaction. The audio does not attempt to dominate the experience, but rather complements the pacing and tone in a subtle, effective way.

The game’s brevity is one of its defining characteristics. A single playthrough can be completed in a relatively short time, making it a concise and focused experience. While multiple endings encourage replay, the overall structure remains consistent, with variations emerging from different dialogue choices rather than entirely new content. This approach aligns with the game’s emphasis on nuance, but it also limits its scope. Players looking for a longer or more mechanically involved experience may find it lacking, as the game relies almost entirely on its narrative and emotional resonance to sustain engagement.

Replayability is present but modest. Exploring different dialogue paths can reveal new perspectives on the conversation, but the changes are often subtle rather than transformative. Each playthrough feels like a variation on a central theme, offering slightly different emotional outcomes rather than entirely new scenarios. This design reinforces the idea that the game is about interpretation and reflection, though it may not provide enough variety to maintain interest over multiple sessions for all players.

In the end, Everything Will Be Fine! succeeds as a focused and introspective piece of interactive storytelling. It demonstrates how a simple premise, when handled with care, can create a meaningful and memorable experience. While its lack of traditional gameplay and limited scope may not appeal to everyone, it offers something distinct for players who appreciate narrative-driven titles that prioritize mood and emotional depth over mechanics. It is a quiet, contemplative journey that lingers not because of what happens, but because of how it makes the player feel.

Rating: 7/10

As it said, its a very short visual novel to the point it makes you wonder if it was worth the money but at the same time, it is an emotionally charged game even if its short and I loved the whole theme, the aesthetics, everything, It's an art form and I would gladly pay the same again.

I really liked this game. There's a couple of typos in english, but that doesnt really matter at all. I really cant figure out how to get the 5th ending though... :(

Edit: A kind stranger enlightened me on how to complete the 5th ending! :)
pretty game cool story. the music is real nice
